meta.title: Unit 3 — Pronouns & I/me (Test 1)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Identify and use singular and plural pronouns correctly in sentences.

questions.question: Which pronoun replaces a female person (she, her)?
questions.explanation: "she" is used to replace a female person's name.

questions.question: "He" is used to replace a male person's name.
questions.explanation: Yes! We use "he" when talking about a male person.

questions.question: Which pronoun goes at the START of a sentence? (I or me)
questions.explanation: "I" is used as the subject (at the start/before the verb). "me" comes after the verb.

questions.question: "I" must always be written as a capital letter.
questions.explanation: Yes! The pronoun "I" is ALWAYS capitalized, no matter where it appears in the sentence.

questions.question: Mina goes on a trip. → ___ visits the desert.
questions.explanation: Mina is a female, so we use "She" as the pronoun.

questions.question: Fill in: ___ am Oliver the otter. (I or me)
questions.hint: This pronoun comes before the verb 'am'
questions.explanation: "I" is the subject — it comes before the verb. "I am Oliver the otter."

questions.question: The desert is hot and dry. → ___ is hot and dry.
questions.explanation: "the desert" is a place/thing — we replace it with "It".

questions.question: Sort these pronouns: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: I/she/it = one person or thing (singular). we/they = more than one (plural).

questions.question: In the sentence "Rosa calls me," the word "me" is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! "me" comes after the verb (as the object). Rosa calls me → "me" is the receiver of the action.

questions.question: Fill in: A lion sees ___. (I or me)
questions.hint: This pronoun comes AFTER the verb 'sees'
questions.explanation: "me" is the object — it comes after the verb. A lion sees me.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "It is soft, but the grass..." (It replaces 'the grass' — a thing.)

questions.question: Emma and I see a snake. → ___ see a snake.
questions.explanation: "Emma and I" = two people (including the speaker) → use "We".

questions.question: In the sentence "I call Rosa," the word "I" is used correctly.
questions.explanation: Yes! "I" is the subject — it comes before the verb. "I call Rosa" is correct.

questions.question: Alex visits the park. → ___ thinks it is great.
questions.explanation: Alex is a male name — we use "He" to replace it.

questions.question: Fill in: Rosa and ___ paint the basket. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "I" is the subject — when combined with another noun (Rosa and I), it's still the subject.

questions.question: Sort these pronouns: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: Note: 'you' can be both singular and plural! he/it = singular. they/we = plural.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'I' and 'me' correctly?
questions.explanation: "I am" is correct — "I" is the subject of the sentence.

questions.question: In the sentence "She always helps I with homework," the pronoun is correct.
questions.explanation: "helps I" is wrong — after a verb, we use "me" (the object): "She always helps me with homework."

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Rosa gives me the basket." 'me' comes after the verb (object).

questions.question: Snakes are dangerous. → ___ are dangerous.
questions.explanation: "Snakes" is plural — we use "They" to replace a plural noun.
